spec_match.rationale: Multiple field configurations (contenteditable, readonly, bidi text) exposed to the new behaviour — visitor sees the clip-on-focus uniformly across them.

interactive.rationale: Four real inputs/contenteditable elements, all focusable. Visitor drives the demo by clicking through.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises diverse field types and a bidirectional case; both render.

edge_cases.rationale: Readonly + contenteditable + bidi is good, but no `direction: rtl` field (the page mentions bidi but uses a latin field with embedded Arabic). Also no `text-overflow: ellipsis ' more'` two-value syntax — that variant also gets clipped on focus per the spec.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colours, no glyph icons, no raw hex.

openQuestions.title: Add a truly RTL field
openQuestions.detail: `<input dir="rtl" lang="ar">` with Arabic content. The ellipsis sits on the left edge of the visual box; the clip-on-focus behaviour should flip to match. That is the most interesting edge case the spec touches.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the two-value text-overflow syntax
openQuestions.detail: `text-overflow: ' more'` and `text-overflow: ellipsis ' more'` are both valid and supported. Add a row that uses each so the visitor can confirm the new clip behaviour fires for the second-value variant too.

openQuestions.title: Show keyboard caret navigation explicitly
openQuestions.detail: Pure click-focus triggers the clip. The spec also triggers on arrow-key caret nav. Add a label saying `press End` and a guide bubble that highlights when the caret moves into the clipped region.

openQuestions.title: Compare with text-overflow: clip baseline
openQuestions.detail: Add a fifth field that uses `text-overflow: clip` (no ellipsis) so visitors see what the new on-focus state collapses to — that's the static baseline against which the change is described.

summary: Useful breadth across field types and good chromestatus reference. The promised bidi case is currently a latin field with Arabic glyphs — the demo needs a true `dir=rtl` field to make the point.
